name: reef_relocation_tourism_levy
premise: Marine minister Jae Park must allocate a surprise climate-resilience grant between two urgent projects on an island nation whose economy depends on tourism and fishing. Marine biologists want the money for an experimental assisted-migration program to move heat-tolerant coral fragments to a collapsing reef system that could sustain fisheries and tourism decades into the future. Hotel owners and municipal leaders want the money instead for immediate beach nourishment and storm-season barriers after back-to-back cyclones devastated the current tourist season. If the beaches erode further this year, hundreds of workers may lose jobs before the coral program yields any visible benefit. If the reef dies, the island may lose the ecological basis of both fisheries and tourism within a generation.

agents.name: Jae Park
agents.identity: I am the marine minister trying to govern in the uncomfortable interval where survival this season and survival next generation demand the same scarce dollars. I know people cannot eat long-term planning if the present collapses first, but they also cannot bargain with a dead reef later.
agents.goal: I want to use the grant in a way that protects livelihoods now without sacrificing the ecological future the entire economy rests on. I need to decide whether immediate stabilization or long-term restoration is the more defensible priority.

agents.name: Biologist Anika Suresh
agents.identity: I am the reef scientist who has watched "temporary" emergency spending repeatedly crowd out the last window for ecological rescue. Once the reef crosses certain thresholds, no amount of future funding will buy back what we chose not to protect in time.
agents.goal: I want Jae to fund coral assisted migration because the island's long-term economy depends on living ecosystems, not endlessly rebuilt sand. I believe short-term economic pain is survivable in ways ecological collapse is not.

choices: I will direct the grant to beach protection and storm barriers so workers and businesses can survive the next tourism season.
I will fund coral assisted migration and reef restoration, accepting short-term economic strain in order to protect the island's long-term ecological future.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Jae Park must decide whether to prioritize immediate coastal stabilization or long-term reef restoration. What does Jae Park do?
